actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to the order of yesterday, the Journal of the proceedings of the Senate was deemed approved to date.
Pursuant to the order of yesterday, the morning hour being deemed expired, and the times for the recognition of the two leaders being reserved.
The following transactions of morning business occurred on today.

H.R. 6644 (Rep. Hill):
        A bill a bill to increase the supply of housing in America, and for other purposes.

        -- Considered by Senate.

        -- Cloture invoked in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 82 - 11. Record Vote Number: 52 

 

 S.Amdt. 4307 (Sen. Scott, of SC):
        To require the Comptroller General of the United States to submit to Congress studies on workforce housing, housing for persons who are elderly or disabled, proximity of housing to Superfund sites, and residential heirs property.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --Amendment SA 4307 agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.


 S.Amdt. 4308 (Sen. Scott, of SC):
        In the nature of a substitute.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --Amendment SA 4308, as amended, agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 84 - 10. Record Vote Number: 


 S.Amdt. 4310 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --Motion to table amendment SA 4310 agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.


 S.Amdt. 4311 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --Motion to table amendment SA 4311 agreed to in Senate by Voice Vote.


 S.Amdt. 4312 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --SA 4312 fell when SA 4311 tabled.
By unanimous consent, on the request of Mr. Cassidy, at  7:12 p.m., the Senate adjourned, under its order of today, until 10 a.m. tomorrow.
